activities_projects.title: Identifying Traits of a Witty Person
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the qualities required to be witty and intelligent
steps: Read the list of personality traits given in the chapter
Select the traits that best describe a witty person
Explain why each selected trait is important
Present your reasoning in class discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical thinking about personality traits
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation of selected traits (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Riddle Interpretation Activity
steps: Read each riddle carefully
Discuss possible meanings with a partner
Write the answers along with reasoning
Compare your answers with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve reasoning and critical thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and explanation of answers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Discussion on Conflict Resolution
activities_projects.objective: Understand how wit and wisdom can resolve conflicts
steps: Discuss how humour can help resolve misunderstandings
Analyse why kings valued witty advisors
Share examples from real life where humour solved a problem
Summarise the group discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand practical uses of wit in real-life situations
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Strategy Analysis of Tenali Rama
steps: Read the section describing the court scene
Identify the strategy Tenali Rama used to make the king realise his mistake
Explain why this strategy was effective
Write a short paragraph analysing the situation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn strategic thinking and problem-solving
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and depth of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play: The Royal Court
steps: Form groups of four or five students
Assign roles such as the king, queen, Tenali Rama, and courtiers
Recreate the scene where Tenali Rama introduces the paddy seeds
Perform the scene for the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop confidence in speaking and acting
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, teamwork, and clarity of dialogue (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary in Context
activities_projects.objective: Understand the meaning of expressions used in the story
steps: Identify phrases such as 'stormed off', 'cast a shadow', and 'hush fell over'
Write their meanings in your own words
Create sentences using each expression
Share sentences with classmates
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct interpretation and sentence formation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sound Words and Tone Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Recognise how sound words convey emotions in storytelling
steps: List sound words used in the chapter
Match them with the correct situations in sentences
Explain how these words help create the mood of the scene
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand expressive language in narratives
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of matching and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Compound Word Formation
activities_projects.objective: Learn how compound words are formed and used
steps: Match words from the two columns provided in the exercise
Form compound words
Use each compound word in a meaningful sentence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand word formation and vocabulary expansion
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct formation and usage of compound words (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Conditional Sentence Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the use of conditional sentences in English
steps: Study the examples of conditional sentences in the chapter
Match subordinate clauses with suitable main clauses
Create three original conditional sentences based on daily life
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve grammar and sentence construction
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct grammar and logical sentence formation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Narrative Essay Writing
activities_projects.objective: Develop narrative writing and reflective thinking
steps: Read the example narrative essay in the chapter
Choose one topic such as 'A Small Act of Kindness that Made a Difference'
Write an essay with introduction, body, and conclusion
Review and edit your essay before submission
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop structured storytelling and writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, organisation, and language accuracy (10 marks)
